text: 🇮🇷 | ResistanceTrench Statement on the 40th Day of the Martyrdom of Ayatollah Imam Khamenei On this solemn day, marking forty days since the martyrdom of Ayatollah Imam Khamenei (may Allah’s mercy be upon him), the Ummah reflects upon a loss that shook the foundations of the Islamic world—yet strengthened its روح of resistance and faith. Indeed, Allah ﷻ is the Ultimate Power, the One who grants victory and عزّة to His sincere servants. The martyrdom of such a leader is not an end, but a continuation of a divine path written by sacrifice, patience, and unwavering ایمان. The Islamic Republic of Iran, through its institutions and especially the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), has reaffirmed that the blood of the martyrs does not weaken the الأمة—it revives it. The legacy of Imam Khamenei lives on through resistance, unity, and steadfastness against oppression and arrogance. His life and martyrdom have become a turning point—fueling determination across the resistance front, from Iran to Palestine and beyond. The enemies sought to silence a voice, but instead ignited a movement that continues to grow stronger with each passing day. The principles he upheld—justice, independence, unity, and defiance against tyranny—remain a guiding light for all those who walk the path of resistance. His legacy is not confined to history; it lives in every act of defiance, every stand for truth, and every believer who refuses submission to falsehood. As emphasized in official statements, including those of the IRGC, the path of the martyrs will not be abandoned. Rather, it will continue with renewed strength under steadfast leadership, and with the وعد of Allah that truth will prevail over falsehood. On this 40th day, ResistanceTrench honors the memory of the martyred leader and all those who stood beside him. The عهد remains unbroken, the path remains clear, and the struggle continues until justice is established. 🫡

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan: The international community must now clearly recognize this reality: as long as Israel’s expansionism continues, it will not be possible to build lasting peace, stability, and security in the Middle East. Israel must be stopped from imposing its geopolitical ambitions on the region through external interventions. The Netanyahu government continues to condemn the people of Gaza to famine under the pretext of war, systematically restricts freedom of worship in East Jerusalem, and adds new unlawful measures each day aimed at undermining the two-state solution in the West Bank. Israel is now extending what it has done in Gaza to Lebanon. Before the ceasefire framework can even be properly established, ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon — carried out without regard for children or civilians — are causing hundreds of casualties and pushing the region into a deeper humanitarian crisis. Ending Israel’s occupation in Lebanon and protecting civilians has become an urgent and non-negotiable priority.

Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene: Trump is mentally unstable.

Survivors of the deadly Iranian drone strike in Kuwait are challenging the Pentagon’s version of events, saying their unit “was unprepared” and not properly protected. One injured soldier rejected the official account, stating: “Painting a picture that ‘one squeaked through’ is a falsehood… the unit was unprepared to provide any defense for itself. It was not a fortified position.” Troops described being stationed at a lightly protected site despite knowing it was a potential target: “We moved closer to Iran, to a deeply unsafe area that was a known target… I don’t think there was a good reason ever articulated.” Some believe the attack could have been avoided. “Telling the truth is important… we’re not going to learn from these mistakes if we pretend these mistakes didn’t happen,” one said, adding the strike was “absolutely” preventable. Source: CBS News

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan: At this stage, it is clear that Syria must be kept away from the negative repercussions of the crisis in our region. The intensive diplomatic engagement we have recently carried out with Syria is fundamentally aimed at this objective.

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇳 Iran has called for the creation of an international fund to compensate governments and nations harmed by unilateral sanctions. Its ambassador to the UN in Geneva condemned sanctions as a tool for pressure and destabilisation, and urged a binding mechanism for compensation by sanctioning countries. @wfwitness

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan on Iran negotiations: It is essential to act with common sense against any known attempts to sabotage the Israeli negotiation process. In particular, the international community must remain alert to potential acts of sabotage by Israel and be prepared to respond appropriately.

German Chancellor Merz: I believe Trump himself did not assume that a country like Iran could be completely wiped out. In my firm conviction, that would not have happened. It was part of his strategy in dealing with Iran.

🇩🇪 🇺🇸 German chancellor Friedrich Merz said he did not want the US-Israeli campaign in Iran to “split” Nato or put further pressure on the relations between the US and its European allies, as he suggested Germany could play a role in securing the strait of Hormuz. “Nato is a guarantor of our security, including and above all in Europe,” he stressed. Merz separately confirmed plans to help with securing the strait of Hormuz, but stressed that any such mission would “ideally” have the backing of the UN security council and of the German parliament, so “no decisions are to be expected within a few days.” @DDGeopolitics

German Chancellor Merz: We did not discuss any withdrawal of U.S. soldiers, nor any restriction on the use of military infrastructure maintained by the American armed forces in Germany. There are stationing agreements in place, and the federal government has no reason to doubt that the American forces have acted within the framework of these agreements in recent weeks.

Iran’s Deputy FM Saeed Khatibzadeh: We were on the verge of responding to a grave violation. Pakistan intervened and conveyed messages that helped prevent further escalation. The United States must choose between war and ceasefire — it cannot have both.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Bloomberg : U.S. President Trump’s top economic adviser, Kevin Hassett, said the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act can be used to impose 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran because the U.S. is “in a state of conflict”. The Supreme Court ruled on 20 February that IEEPA does not authorise the president to impose tariffs, without carving out an exception for wartime. The court was split on whether the president has inherent wartime tariff authority. @wfwitness
German Chancellor Merz: We are now resuming talks with Tehran. We are doing this in coordination with the United States and our European partners.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇷🇺 🇨🇳 🇹🇼 WSJ : A global scramble is under way to protect submarine cables from potential sabotage. NATO is using ships and drones in the Baltic Sea after a Russia‑linked vessel cut vital cables in 2024. The UK exposed what it called a secret month‑long operation by Russian submarines targeting underwater infrastructure. Taiwan has increased coast guard patrols and penalties for damaging cables after several incidents involving Chinese vessels. The conflict in Iran has raised new concerns that cable‑laying and repair ships may not be able to transit the Strait of Hormuz, delaying fixes. @wfwitness

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇷🇺 According to The Moscow Times, Russian authorities are considering canceling the May 9 Victory Day parades in Moscow and St. Petersburg due to potential threats from new Ukrainian long-range missiles. 📌 Officials warn that even the announcement of a missile threat during the parade would cause major disruption and damage Russia’s image, even if no strike occurs. 🟥 This could mark the first time in 30 years that the parade is not held, with pro-war bloggers citing Ukrainian missiles capable of reaching Moscow within 10–30 minutes. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

OSINTdefender RT @sentdefender : Today, UK Defense Secretary John Healey announced that Russia has been conducting covert undersea operations near its critical undersea infrastructure north of the nation in the last few months. According to Secretary Healey, the operation involved one Russian Akula-class attack submarine and two Russian submarines associated with Russia’s Main Directorate of Deep-Sea Research (GUGI). According to Healey, a Royal Navy warship and a Royal Air Force P-8 were deployed to monitor all three submarines, after which the Akula-class sub retreated from the area, but according to the secretary, the two GUGI-associated submarines remain on task in the area and are currently being monitored. tweet

Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz: Hezbollah, the terrorist organization, is pleading for a ceasefire, and its Iranian patrons are also pressuring and threatening—out of serious fear that Israel will crush Hezbollah. The IDF is prepared and ready to act with force if Iran fires toward Israel.

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics: "🇮🇷 Security official: If attacks against Lebanon do not stop, there will be no negotiations A senior security official told Tehran Times: ➡️Stopping the war against Hezbollah has been, and will remain, an important part of Iran’s plan. ➡️If attacks against…" 🇮🇷 Informed source: Iran considers negotiations meaningless without Lebanon being included in a ceasefire ➡️ An informed source told Fars News Agency, referring to speculation about the resumption of negotiations, that “the issue of Lebanon and a ceasefire in that country is a definite and non-negotiable precondition of the Islamic Republic of Iran for entering any new negotiation process.” ➡️ The source added that “Pakistan’s mediating proposal is also based on this,” and said: “Iran’s 10-point plan, which has been communicated to international intermediaries, includes a clear and explicit clause regarding the necessity of including Lebanon as part of a comprehensive ceasefire. Tehran did not accept the ceasefire until the American side and President Trump had officially acknowledged acceptance of the full 10-point package. Therefore, under conditions of continued attacks on Lebanon, it considers negotiations strategically meaningless and unproductive.” ➡️ The source also said that all senior officials of the Islamic Republic of Iran are in full agreement on this issue. This internal consensus means Tehran’s message is unified and clear: if the issue of Lebanon is not resolved, there will be no negotiations. ➡️ The informed source further referred to countermeasures planned in the scenario of a failure to achieve a ceasefire in Lebanon, saying: “The country’s diplomatic and military apparatus does not rely on a single option. It uses multiple options to ensure the inclusion of Lebanon in the ceasefire is secured.” 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
